jokes.text: The word is superstition, not 'stupid-stition.'
jokes.setup: Charlie mispronounces 'superstition' as 'stupid-stition'
jokes.punchline: Another character corrects him with the proper word
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Charlie's verbal fumbling - economical wordplay that reveals character

jokes.text: I'm not a Cowboy fan, I'm a Tony Romo fan. And he retired, so now I bleed green.
jokes.setup: Mac needs to defend his Eagles fandom
jokes.punchline: His loyalty was to a specific retired player, not the team
jokes.explanation: Absurd sports fan logic that's perfectly in character - the shallow, bandwagon nature is funny

jokes.text: Do you know how football works?
jokes.setup: Charlie insists fans affect game outcomes
jokes.punchline: Deadpan question about basic sports reality
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective straight-man response to Charlie's delusion

jokes.text: What's going on with your eye?
jokes.setup: Someone notices Dee's eye is acting strange
jokes.punchline: Visual of her obvious discomfort/twitching
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup, likely building to something bigger

jokes.text: I just, I slept on it funny.
jokes.setup: Obvious excuse for clearly problematic eye situation
jokes.punchline: Weak lie that doesn't explain the severity
jokes.explanation: Typical deflection that makes the situation more suspicious

jokes.text: how is 'salmonella' the only word you spelled correctly?
jokes.setup: Charlie made a sign with spelling errors
jokes.punchline: The most difficult word is somehow correct while simple words are wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his weird competencies in unexpected areas while failing at basics

jokes.text: Are we closed for salmonella, or are we closed 'cause of the Super Bowl? It's very confusing.
jokes.setup: Sign claims two different reasons for being closed
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of both being true simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that highlights the absurdity of Charlie's logic

jokes.text: I wear green. I don't eat it. I'm green man. I eat brown, preferably, uh, milksteak with a little light char to it.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains his superstitious color system
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, nonsensical food preferences based on color
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie absurdism with callback to his famous milksteak obsession - perfect character integration

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish you'd just disappear.
jokes.setup: Frustrated comment about Charlie being difficult
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly sets up the entire episode premise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural setup that becomes the episode's central premise - excellent earned irony

jokes.text: Where the hell is green man?!
jokes.setup: Charlie finally finds his suit but everyone's gone
jokes.punchline: Screaming about costume to empty space
jokes.explanation: Good transition moment showing Charlie's priorities remain skewed even when alone

jokes.text: I made my friends disappear.
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes he's alone after wishing they'd disappear
jokes.punchline: Takes credit for magical powers he doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - connecting unrelated events in his delusional worldview

jokes.text: I made my friends disappear!
jokes.setup: Repeats the same realization
jokes.punchline: With more excitement and conviction
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the previous joke, showing Charlie's growing delusion

jokes.text: Hey, Frank! I'm going through your stuff!
jokes.setup: Charlie announces his invasion of privacy
jokes.punchline: Childlike glee at misbehaving with no consequences
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's regression to child-like behavior when unsupervised

jokes.text: I got your... sticky Bible!
jokes.setup: Charlie going through Mac's religious items
jokes.punchline: Discovering something inappropriate about Mac's Bible
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Mac's religious hypocrisy, economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Dee, woof!
jokes.setup: Charlie going through Dee's belongings
jokes.punchline: Reacting with dog sound to something he found
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I don't know why I'm screaming. This doesn't hurt at all.
jokes.setup: Charlie apparently stepped on something painful
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's screaming for no reason
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy subversion - sets up pain expectation then deflates it

jokes.text: Really cool, too, man. Those, like, those uniforms look really cool on you. You guys look, like, really strong.
jokes.setup: Charlie talking to TV players like real people
jokes.punchline: Awkward compliments about their appearance
jokes.explanation: Charlie's social awkwardness and delusion on full display - in character but not economical

jokes.text: Well, which one is it? Is it closed for salmonella, or is it closed for the Super Bowl?
jokes.setup: Criminal reads Charlie's confusing sign
jokes.punchline: Even burglars are confused by Charlie's logic
jokes.explanation: Good callback that gets funnier when even criminals are bothered by the inconsistency

jokes.text: I have to defend it.
jokes.setup: Burglars are in the bar
jokes.punchline: Charlie takes heroic stance despite being completely unprepared
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Home Alone parody elements - Charlie as unlikely hero

jokes.text: Ooh, ooh, that's a gusher. I got to stop the bleeding.
jokes.setup: Charlie apparently hurt himself setting traps
jokes.punchline: Casual, almost excited reaction to serious injury
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Charlie's weird detachment from pain - sets up his deteriorating state

jokes.text: How did I lose an entire day?!
jokes.setup: Time jump to game day
jokes.punchline: Charlie has no memory of the missing time
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's deteriorating mental state - time distortion from injury/stress

jokes.text: I-I didn't do my color rituals. Guys, you're gonna lose the game because of me!
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes he missed his superstitious routine
jokes.punchline: Takes personal responsibility for professional sports outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie delusion - his narcissistic belief in his cosmic importance to sports

jokes.text: and brown.
jokes.setup: Charlie describing a rat he's caught
jokes.punchline: Ominous focus on the rat being brown (his ritual color for eating)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup that makes the audience realize what's coming - perfect economy and dark escalation

jokes.text: Ah. The brown is down. The brown is down.
jokes.setup: Charlie following through on eating the rat
jokes.punchline: Treating rat consumption like a sports announcement
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - Charlie's cheerful sports commentary about eating vermin is perfectly absurd and earned

jokes.text: Eagles must have scored. It's working!
jokes.setup: Charlie connects rat eating to game events
jokes.punchline: Believes his disgusting act had cosmic sports impact
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Charlie's delusional system - his joy at 'successful' rat eating is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Although I got a feeling that's gonna come back to haunt me later.
jokes.setup: Charlie's moment of clarity about eating rat
jokes.punchline: Understated prediction of obvious consequences
jokes.explanation: Good setup for immediate payoff - Charlie's rare moment of logical foresight

jokes.text: No, right now.
jokes.setup: Charlie predicts consequences 'later'
jokes.punchline: Immediate onset of rat poisoning/sickness
jokes.explanation: Perfect rapid-fire setup/payoff pair - economical escalation with great timing

jokes.text: Never, ever, ever, ever, ever eat a rat.
jokes.setup: Charlie experiences immediate consequences
jokes.punchline: Delivers obvious life advice with unnecessary emphasis
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up - Charlie stating the incredibly obvious with earnest conviction

jokes.text: Soar like an Eagle!
jokes.setup: Charlie throwing shoes to reach paint can
jokes.punchline: Connecting mundane action to team metaphor
jokes.explanation: Charlie applying team spirit to desperate physical comedy situation

jokes.text: Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Charlie realizes he needs yellow liquid and has no beer
jokes.punchline: Dawning horror of what he'll have to drink instead
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for the obvious gross-out moment to come

jokes.text: You drink that yellow.
jokes.setup: Charlie's hallucination encouraging him
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic support for drinking urine
jokes.explanation: Charlie's hallucination maintains his delusional commitment to the ritual system

jokes.text: Use your thumbs.
jokes.setup: Charlie trapped and suffering
jokes.punchline: Simple solution that was always available
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - the solution was obvious but Charlie needed to hallucinate it

jokes.text: Well, why didn't you tell me there was a release lever?!
jokes.setup: Charlie angry at his hallucination
jokes.punchline: Blaming his own subconscious for not helping sooner
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - being mad at his own hallucination for withholding information

jokes.text: You bought the trap, Charlie. I only know about the release lever because you know about the release lever.
jokes.setup: Charlie's anger at his hallucination
jokes.punchline: Hallucination explains the logic of being Charlie's subconscious
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy that explains hallucination logic while being absurd - sophisticated writing

jokes.text: We're-we're not real.
jokes.setup: Charlie not understanding hallucination logic
jokes.punchline: Blunt statement of obvious unreality
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that explicitly states what the audience already knows

jokes.text: you're gonna have to get back in that trap.
jokes.setup: Game situation has changed
jokes.punchline: Must re-injure himself for superstitious purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Charlie's superstitious logic - must recreate exact conditions including injury

jokes.text: Does it have to be the same leg or...
jokes.setup: Must get back in trap
jokes.punchline: Practical question about superstitious requirement specifics
jokes.explanation: Excellent Charlie moment - treating absurd situation with practical logistics concerns

jokes.text: Every single thing that every fan does, at home or at the stadium, makes a direct impact in the game.
jokes.setup: Charlie questioning if this will work
jokes.punchline: Elaborate justification of impossible sports fan influence
jokes.explanation: Good reinforcement of Charlie's core delusion about fan impact on professional sports
